sectionsModified: Configure ASP.NET Identity to work with the modified ApplicationUser (code sample: NoOpApplicationRole → ApplicationRole, NoOpApplicationRoleStore → ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>)
docFactsUsed: AREA-002: ApplicationRole maps to MemberRoleInfo similarly to how ApplicationUser maps to MemberInfo
notes: Updated both code sample locations in the Identity configuration block: NoOpApplicationRole → ApplicationRole and NoOpApplicationRoleStore → ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>. Added new 'Remarks – ApplicationRole and MemberRoleInfo' section after the existing 'Remarks – ApplicationUser and MemberInfo' section, mirroring its structure to explain the bidirectional mapping between ApplicationRole and MemberRoleInfo, and the role of ApplicationRoleStore. Added member_roles_xp to related_pages and linked to the Member roles page for further reading. All type names use backtick formatting per INV-formatting-004 and REC-015. No type names are pluralized directly. Terminology follows REC-013 (member, visitor, sign in). Code sample is compile-ready and consistent with the parent registration-and-authentication.md page (TASK-003) and the member-roles.md page (TASK-001).

invariantSatisfactionDetails.INV-formatting-004: All API members in backticks: ApplicationRole, ApplicationRoleStore<TRole>, ApplicationRoleStore<ApplicationRole>, IdentityRole<int>, MemberRoleInfo, MapFromMemberRoleInfo, MapToMemberRoleInfo, RoleManager, IRoleStore<TRole>, ApplicationUser, MemberInfo, ExtendedApplicationUser, etc.
invariantSatisfactionDetails.INV-codesamples-010: Code sample is a single logical block showing the complete Identity configuration with user store, role store, user manager, and sign-in manager — appropriate for a how-to guide. Consistent with the parent page's code sample.
invariantSatisfactionDetails.INV-structure-011: Article remains self-contained. The new section links to Member roles (member_roles_xp) with proper page_link syntax and adds the identifier to related_pages. The existing link to Registration and authentication (tYouCw) is preserved.
